title: PostgreSQL Metadata, Outbox Events, and Orphan Repair · TypeScript Build Room
summary: A TypeScript pairing room incrementally builds typed metadata and object reconciliation model, validates one changed runtime input, and keeps earlier HTTP, accessibility, security, stream, AbortSignal, and resource-budget rules active. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of typed metadata and object reconciliation model while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, assign authoritative fields to application database, S3 object state, tags, events, or derived indexes and document reconciliation.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, persist idempotent intent, reservation, object identity, expected integrity, upload, quarantine, publish, failure, and expiry transitions.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, choose database-first, object-first, or direct-upload ordering and expose each crash window, compensation, and user-visible state.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop System Of Record Boundary
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop TypeScript file-delivery team handles case 6860 during a AWS SDK v3 dependency canary. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to expose PostgreSQL and S3 crash windows through an exhaustive workflow-state result; do not treat TypeScript types, a resolved promise, CORS, ETag, or an emulator as stronger proof than it is; then abort during a stream or multipart promise and prove every task and part reaches a terminal state. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, assign authoritative fields to application database, S3 object state, tags, events, or derived indexes and document reconciliation.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop System Of Record Boundary.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for in a strict node 24 and typescript 7 service, assign authoritative fields to application database, s3 object state, tags, events, or derived indexes and document reconciliation. For S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop System Of Record Boundary, keep System Of Record Boundary connected to executable TypeScript storage and delivery decision evidence.

Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Orphan Repair
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-TypeScript function headed "// Evidence: storage-ts-orphan-repair-16m650" that uses case 9808 to expose PostgreSQL and S3 crash windows through an exhaustive workflow-state result. Guided rehearsal: predict the branch, make one bounded increment, and inspect peer-readable changed output. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import Node or AWS SDK packages, open sockets, contact S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, networks, or external effects, read host state, execute host commands, or use credentials; verify those boundaries later with the TypeScript 7.0.2 native compiler and the @typescript/typescript6 package 6.0.2 compiler that reports 6.0.3, Node 24.18.0, AWS SDK v3.1087.0, controlled AWS, stream and handle, load, fault, security, cost, restore, and production gates.
